signature: def solve(subject: str, resources: list[str], action: str, policies: list[str]) -> list[str]:

signature: def solve(subject: str, resource: str, action: str, policies: list[str]) -> list[str]:
prompt: Implement solve(subject, resource, action, policies). Evaluate authorization for the action and also for the next more privileged action (if any), returning a list of audit log strings. The more privileged action is the one with the next higher priority after the given action; if there is none, return a list with only the original audit log.
visible_tests: assert solve("role=director;department=ops;level=5", "type=record;sensitivity=high;owner=eng", "write", ['(owner != "ops") -> allow', '(owner == "sales") -> allow'])[0] == "[director/ops/L5] write record:high:eng -> ALLOW"
hidden_tests: assert all(isinstance(x, str) for x in solve('role=engineer;department=eng;level=2', 'type=doc;sensitivity=low;owner=eng', 'read', ['(department == owner) -> allow']))

prompt: Implement solve(histories). Suggest a role for each access history and return the most common suggestion. If there is a tie, return the one that appears first in the suggestion list. Return 'intern' for empty input.

signature: def solve(subjects: list[str], resource: str, policies: list[str]) -> str | None:
prompt: Implement solve(subjects, resource, policies). Return the subject string that is allowed to perform the most distinct actions on the resource. In case of a tie, return the first such subject. Return None if no subject is allowed any action.
Implement solve(policies). Return True if there exists at least one role that appears in both an allow policy and a deny policy (a potential conflict).
visible_tests: assert solve(['(role == "manager") -> allow', '(role == "manager") -> deny', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) is True
assert solve(['(role == "engineer") -> allow', '(role == "engineer") -> deny']) is True
hidden_tests: assert solve(['(role == "admin") -> allow', '(role == "intern") -> deny']) is False
assert solve(['(role == "manager") -> allow', '(role == "manager") -> deny', '(role == "director") -> allow']) is True
assert solve(['(department == "eng") -> allow', '(role == "engineer") -> deny']) is False
assert solve(['(role == "director") -> allow', '(role == "director") -> deny', '(role == "director") -> deny']) is True

prompt: Implement solve(policies). Return True if there exists at least one role that appears in both an allow policy and a deny policy (a potential conflict).
visible_tests: assert solve(['(role == "manager") -> allow', '(role == "manager") -> deny', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) is True
assert solve(['(role == "engineer") -> allow', '(role == "engineer") -> deny']) is True
hidden_tests: assert solve(['(role == "admin") -> allow', '(role == "intern") -> deny']) is False
assert solve(['(role == "manager") -> allow', '(role == "manager") -> deny', '(role == "director") -> allow']) is True
assert solve(['(department == "eng") -> allow', '(role == "engineer") -> deny']) is False
assert solve(['(role == "director") -> allow', '(role == "director") -> deny', '(role == "director") -> deny']) is True

signature: def solve(subject: str, resources: list[str], policies: list[str]) -> dict:

signature: def solve(subject: str, resources: list[str], policies: list[str]) -> dict:
prompt: Implement solve(subject, resources, policies). Return a report dictionary with keys 'total', 'allowed', and 'denied' summarizing read access for the subject across the resources.

signature: def solve(subject: str, policies: list[str]) -> dict[str, bool]:
prompt: Implement solve(subject, policies). Return a dictionary mapping each sensitivity level ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical') to whether the subject is allowed to read a document with that sensitivity.
visible_tests: assert solve("role=manager;department=ops;level=3", ['(level >= 3 and sensitivity != "critical") -> allow', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': False}
assert solve("role=admin;department=ops;level=5", ['(role == "admin") -> allow']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': True}
hidden_tests: assert solve('role=intern;department=eng;level=1', ['(level >= 2) -> allow']) == {'low': False, 'medium': False, 'high': False, 'critical': False}
assert solve('role=manager;department=sales;level=3', ['(level >= 3) -> allow', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': False}
assert solve('role=engineer;department=hr;level=2', ['(sensitivity != "critical") -> allow']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': False}
assert solve('role=director;department=legal;level=5', ['(role == "director") -> allow', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': False}
assert solve('role=manager;department=eng;level=3', ['(department == "eng") -> allow', '(sensitivity == "critical") -> deny']) == {'low': True, 'medium': True, 'high': True, 'critical': False}

prompt: Implement solve(series). Parse the Nimbla series and return its last numeric value.
Implement solve(series). Parse the Nimbla series and return the list of timestamps.
Implement solve(series). Parse the Nimbla series and return the list of numeric values.
visible_tests: assert len(solve('NIM|41:G5,46:E3,51:F1')) == 3 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|41:G5,46:E3,51:F1'), [7.5, 5.3, 6.1]))
hidden_tests: assert len(solve('NIM|1:A7')) == 1 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:A7'), [1.7]))
assert len(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:B2')) == 2 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:B2'), [1.1, 2.2]))
assert len(solve('NIM|1:G9,2:A0,3:C3')) == 3 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:G9,2:A0,3:C3'), [7.9, 1.0, 3.3]))
assert len(solve('NIM|5:D5,10:E6')) == 2 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|5:D5,10:E6'), [4.5, 5.6]))

signature: def solve(series: str, start: int, end: int) -> list[float]:
prompt: Implement solve(series, start, end). Parse the series, select entries with timestamps in [start, end], and return their min-max normalized values.
hidden_tests: assert len(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:B2,3:C3,4:D4', 2, 3)) == 2 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:B2,3:C3,4:D4', 2, 3), [0.0, 1.0]))
assert len(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:A1,3:A1', 1, 3)) == 3 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:A1,2:A1,3:A1', 1, 3), [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]))
assert len(solve('NIM|1:A0,2:G9', 1, 2)) == 2 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|1:A0,2:G9', 1, 2), [0.0, 1.0]))
assert len(solve('NIM|10:A1,20:B2,30:C3', 15, 25)) == 1 and all(abs(a - b) < 1e-9 for a, b in zip(solve('NIM|10:A1,20:B2,30:C3', 15, 25), [0.0]))
